title: LRS Graph Data Model Explorer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Learning Analytics / Data Engineering
description: Interactive vis-network diagram of the Learning Record Store graph data model. Every node label and relationship from the specification appears in the graph. Shows why the model has no per-statement vertex: statements live in the event store and are compressed into one summary vertex per analytical grain.

educational.topic: Graph data modeling for high-volume learning analytics
learningObjectives: Analyze how the entities of a learning-analytics graph relate to one another
Identify the properties and controlled vocabularies each entity and relationship carries
Explain why per-statement vertices are prohibited at 10,000 statements/sec, using the arithmetic
Describe the grain of each summary vertex and what determines its compression ratio
Trace how evidence is attributed to a textbook version without storing individual statements in the graph

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive exploration built around a quantitative discrepancy — the learner computes the cost of the naive one-vertex-per-event model and discovers why the specification forbids it
keyQuestions: Why is there no Statement vertex in this model?
Where do the raw xAPI statements actually live, and are they lost?
A student views a page 50 times. How many PageEngagement vertices exist afterward?
Why does SectionRollup compress ~3,000:1 while QuestionResponse compresses only ~3:1?
commonMisconceptions: That a graph database should store every event as a vertex — the natural first model, and the one this MicroSim exists to correct
That compression discards data — it does not; the full statements stay queryable in the event store, and compression decides only what is materialized in the graph
That the compression ratio is a fixed property of the system — it is a property of the grain, which is why it ranges from ~3:1 to ~3,000:1 across the six summary vertices
That the dashed red cylinder is a graph vertex — it is drawn to show where statements live, precisely because they are not in the graph
assessmentOpportunities: Compute the daily vertex count and write rate implied by one-vertex-per-statement at the specified ingest rate
Name the six summary vertices and the grain each is keyed on
Explain what statements_compressed records and why every summary carries it
